content:
6 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The JavaScript date API that every developer hates has now become legacy. A new API has launched that solves all the issues of the old date API. The new API’s name is Temporal. Let’s see how it works.
Link for free (non-member) readers
One quick note before we start. Temporal is still a TC39 proposal at Stage 3, so it isn’t shipped natively everywhere yet. If you want to run the code examples below in your own project right now, install the polyfill first.
npm install @js-temporal/polyfillimport { Temporal } from '@js-temporal/polyfill';Once native support lands in your browser or Node version, you can drop the polyfill and use the global Temporal object directly.
All practical examples here:

Issue 1: Everything Comes Bundled Together
The old Date object gives you everything at once. Time, date, time zone, all of it lands in a single object whether you asked for it or not…
